constraintLeaderboard.description: Critical authentication and routing infrastructure failures causing 401/404 errors across the entire application
constraintLeaderboard.goal: Reliability of LIVE features — Clock-in/out, geofencing, scheduling, payroll reports, task assignment, and Clerk auth MUST work flawlessly
evidenceFromCrawl: GET /api/auth/session → 401 (auth check failing for legitimate users)
Multiple dashboard pages return empty/error states due to failed API calls
unblocks: Clock-in/out functionality becomes accessible to authenticated users
Schedule management and viewing becomes possible
Task assignment and tracking can function
Payroll report generation becomes available
Activity logging and audit trails work correctly
All dashboard and admin functionality becomes usable

constraintLeaderboard.description: Critical authentication and database connection failures are blocking ALL app functionality. Clerk auth integration is misconfigured causing 401 errors across the application, and database queries are failing with connection errors.
constraintLeaderboard.goal: Reliability of LIVE features — Clock-in/out, geofencing, scheduling, payroll reports, task assignment, and Clerk auth MUST work flawlessly
evidenceFromCrawl: Multiple pages failing to load due to auth failures
Database connection timeouts in server logs
Failed API requests returning 500 errors due to DB connection issues
Users unable to access protected routes
unblocks: Users can successfully log in and access the application
Clock-in/out functionality becomes accessible
Schedule viewing and task management becomes operational
Payroll reports can be generated
All API endpoints can serve authenticated requests
Database queries can execute successfully

constraintLeaderboard.description: Geofencing validation is completely broken — employees can clock in from anywhere, violating legal time-tracking compliance requirements
constraintLeaderboard.goal: Reliability of LIVE features — Clock-in/out, geofencing, scheduling, payroll reports, task assignment, and Clerk auth MUST work flawlessly
evidenceFromCrawl: POST /api/time-entries/clock-in returns 200 success even when user is 5000+ meters from store location
Geofence validation function exists but result is ignored in the route handler
No error logging when geofence check fails
Database allows time_entries to be created without location verification
Employee can clock in from home, work 8 hours at store, clock out from home — system accepts all events
unblocks: Legal compliance for time tracking
Trust in payroll accuracy
Ability to enforce on-site work requirements
Foundation for future geo-based features (shift reminders, proximity alerts)
Manager confidence in reported work hours

constraintLeaderboard.description: Critical authentication and authorization failures blocking all protected routes. Multiple 401/403 errors across core features (clock-in, schedule management, payroll, tasks) prevent users from accessing the app's primary functionality.
constraintLeaderboard.goal: Reliability of LIVE features — Clock-in/out, geofencing, scheduling, payroll reports, task assignment, and Clerk auth MUST work flawlessly
evidenceFromCrawl: Pattern: 100% of protected API routes returning 401/403 errors
unblocks: Clock-in/out functionality becomes accessible
Schedule viewing and shift management becomes operational
Payroll reports become accessible to authorized users
Task assignment and tracking becomes functional
All downstream LIVE features that depend on authenticated requests
User onboarding and member directory access
Activity logging and audit trail functionality

constraintLeaderboard.description: Critical authentication and database connection failures causing 500 errors across all protected routes
constraintLeaderboard.goal: Reliability of LIVE features — Clock-in/out, geofencing, scheduling, payroll reports, task assignment, and Clerk auth MUST work flawlessly
evidenceFromCrawl: Multiple routes failing with identical 500 pattern suggests systemic auth or database issue
No working protected routes discovered in crawl — complete failure of core functionality
unblocks: Clock-in/out becomes reliable

failedAttempts.constraint: Geofencing validation is completely broken — employees can clock in from anywhere, violating legal time-tracking compliance requirements
failedAttempts.reason: Geofencing validation constraint is NOT resolved. The constraint requires that POST /api/time-entries/clock-in returns 403 when user is >100m from store, but the codebase provided shows NO geofence validation implementation in the time entries route handler. The crawl verification only checks that pages load (which they do), but does NOT verify the actual geofence enforcement behavior. The re-crawl results show WebSocket connection failures and page load issues, but these are separate from the geofencing constraint. Critical: zero of the 6 planned steps have been completed.

activeConstraint.description: Critical authentication and database connection failures causing 500 errors across all protected routes
activeConstraint.why: Every LIVE feature depends on auth and database access. When Clerk auth or database connections fail, the entire app becomes unusable. This is the root blocker preventing users from clocking in, viewing schedules, managing tasks, or accessing any protected functionality.
unblocks: Clock-in/out becomes reliable
Schedule viewing and management works consistently
Task assignment and tracking functions properly
Payroll reports can be generated without errors
API performance can be measured accurately (can't optimize what doesn't work)
Shopify sync can proceed (depends on database writes)
User trust is restored (no more random 500 errors)
evidenceFromCrawl: Multiple routes failing with identical 500 pattern suggests systemic auth or database issue
No working protected routes discovered in crawl — complete failure of core functionality
activeConstraint.goal: Reliability of LIVE features — Clock-in/out, geofencing, scheduling, payroll reports, task assignment, and Clerk auth MUST work flawlessly

steps.description: Add comprehensive error handling and logging to Clerk auth middleware. Catch ClerkExpressRequireAuth failures, log specific error types (expired token, invalid token, network failure), return proper 401/500 responses with structured error messages instead of letting errors bubble up unhandled.
successCriteria: Auth middleware catches all Clerk errors
Returns 401 for auth failures with clear error message
Returns 500 for Clerk API unavailability with retry guidance
Logs structured auth errors to console for debugging

steps.description: Add database connection health checking, retry logic with exponential backoff, and connection pool monitoring. Wrap all db queries in try/catch that logs connection state. Add a /health endpoint check for database connectivity. Ensure graceful degradation if db is temporarily unavailable.
successCriteria: Database connection failures are caught and logged with connection state
Failed queries retry 3x with exponential backoff before failing
Connection pool exhaustion is detected and logged
Health check endpoint reports database status

steps.description: Wrap all route handlers in try/catch blocks. Add input validation with Zod. Return structured error responses with proper HTTP status codes. Log all errors with request context (userId, endpoint, timestamp). Handle database query failures gracefully with user-friendly error messages.
successCriteria: All handlers have try/catch
All inputs validated with Zod schemas
All errors return JSON with {success: false, error: {message, code}}
Database errors return 500 with 'Database temporarily unavailable' message
Auth errors return 401 with 'Authentication required' message

steps.description: Apply same error handling pattern as time-entries: try/catch all handlers, Zod input validation, structured error responses, proper status codes, comprehensive logging with request context.
successCriteria: All handlers have try/catch
All inputs validated with Zod
Structured error responses on all paths
Database and auth errors handled gracefully

steps.description: Apply same error handling pattern: try/catch all handlers, Zod input validation, structured error responses, proper status codes, comprehensive logging.
successCriteria: All handlers have try/catch
All inputs validated with Zod
Structured error responses on all paths

steps.description: Apply same error handling pattern: try/catch all handlers, Zod input validation, structured error responses, proper status codes, comprehensive logging.

steps.description: Apply same error handling pattern: try/catch all handlers, Zod input validation, structured error responses, proper status codes, comprehensive logging. Extra care on payroll calculations — add decimal arithmetic validation.
successCriteria: All handlers have try/catch
All inputs validated with Zod
Structured error responses on all paths
Payroll calculations use decimal.js or integer cents
Database and auth errors handled gracefully

steps.description: Add global error handler middleware at the end of the Express middleware stack. Catch any unhandled errors, log them with full stack trace and request context, return structured 500 response. Add process-level unhandled rejection and uncaught exception handlers that log and attempt graceful shutdown.
successCriteria: Global error handler catches all unhandled route errors
Returns structured 500 JSON response
Logs errors with stack trace, request URL, user ID, timestamp
Process handlers prevent silent crashes

steps.description: Create structured logging utility to replace console.log. Include log levels (debug, info, warn, error), request context injection, timestamp formatting, and optional JSON output for production. Export logger instance to use across all routes and middleware.
successCriteria: Logger supports debug/info/warn/error levels
Includes timestamps on all log entries
Can inject request context (userId, endpoint, method)
JSON output mode for production logs

steps.description: Create comprehensive health check endpoint that tests: database connectivity, Clerk API reachability, basic query performance, available connection pool slots. Return structured health status with individual component checks. Used by monitoring and deployment health checks.
successCriteria: GET /health returns 200 when all systems healthy
Returns 503 with component breakdown when any system unhealthy
Tests database with simple query
Reports connection pool status
Response includes timestamp and version info

activeConstraint.completionCriteria: All protected API routes return either successful data (200) or proper error responses (401/403/500) with structured JSON. No unhandled 500 errors in crawl. All LIVE features load without internal server errors. Health endpoint reports all systems operational.
